personalInfo.name: Chief Supt. Avi Amar, of blessed memory
personalInfo.quote: Don't worry, we'll get through this, I'm protecting you.

events.title: Childhood in Moshav Otzem
events.description: Born on December 18, 1968, in Moshav Otzem in the Lachish region, the son of Chana and Yisrael. The third child with ten brothers and sisters: Lucy, Ora, Rucha, Sofia, Lior, Bat Sheva, Michal, Miro, Yael, and Asaf. A smiling, blue-eyed, and pleasant child, who stood out for his good virtues, honesty, and responsibility, always being the first to get up and offer help. Following his leadership skills, he was accepted to study at a military boarding school for command.

events.title: Naval Commando & Givati Service
events.description: In 1987, he enlisted in the IDF and served as a combat soldier in 'Shayetet 13', the Navy's elite commando unit. He went through the grueling training course and participated in complex military operations. He later transferred to the Givati Brigade, completed the officers' course, and served as a highly esteemed Company Commander.

events.title: Israel Police & Academic Development
events.description: On October 17, 1994, he enlisted in the Israel Police. Over the years, he served as a Counter-Terrorism Action Officer (Lotar), District Marksman Officer, and Detention Center Commander. His final role was Commander of the Infrastructure Branch in the 'Yoav' Unit for spatial enforcement in the Negev and counter-terrorism. Simultaneously, he completed a BA in Criminology and an MA in Middle Eastern History, demonstrating professionalism and uncompromising dedication.

events.title: The Most Devoted Father and Husband
events.description: Husband to Pazit. Over the years, Avi had six children: Stav, Shachar, and Itay from a previous marriage, and Efrat, Adel, and Ofek from his marriage to Pazit (his son Or was born half a year after he fell). Despite his demanding work, he made sure to make time for meaningful conversations and absolute involvement in raising his children. A highly energetic man with 'golden hands', he was the pillar of his entire extended family.

events.title: "The Horse Whisperer"
events.description: Beyond all his occupations and obligations, his main hobby was raising and riding horses. He tended to the horses in his stable with immense devotion, gave them medical care, and knew how to train even the most stubborn horse. 'The Horse Whisperer', that's what his friends called him.

events.title: Long-Distance Running
events.description: Another hobby he picked up was long-distance running. Among others, he participated in marathons in Tiberias, Jerusalem, Tel Aviv, and Eilat, the Rome Marathon, and an ultra-marathon where he ran eighty kilometers. He was also a die-hard fan of the 'Maccabi' Tel Aviv basketball team and enjoyed watching their games.

events.title: Fighting in Sderot and Kfar Aza
events.description: Awoke to the sirens, and at 6:50 AM, following the 'Parash Peleshet' order, he went out to fight. He fought at Sa'ad Junction and Sderot, neutralized a terrorist squad, and continued to Kfar Aza. There he rescued residents under fire and prevented infiltration into the communities. While securing weapons from terrorist vehicles, he remembered to stop at a gas station to get water for his thirsty officers - calling his friend Nadir, the store owner, and asking him to charge him. In this inferno, a photo was taken of him hugging a terrified soldier and whispering: 'I am protecting you'.

events.title: The Final Battle
events.description: Rushing from there to Kibbutz Be'eri, he joined the MATPA police force in armored 'Caracal' vehicles. Avi insisted on replacing one of the officers sitting in the vehicle, and entered the kibbutz with him to clear out terrorists. The vehicles suffered an ambush of RPG missiles. Avi fell in this battle at 14:29 alongside seven heroic police officers. He was posthumously promoted to the rank of Chief Superintendent.

events.title: Sanctity of Life and Zionism
events.description: His legacy continues to shine in every way: The memorial site in the Wadi is named after him. A Torah scroll was introduced in his memory in Otzem. Maccabi Tel Aviv held a tribute ceremony for him, and his son Itay is raising funds for an MDA intensive care ambulance in light of the education on the sanctity of life he received from his father. His name is engraved in stone at the Police Memorial and the Police Heritage Centre.

lastPhoto.title: The Final Photograph of Chief Supt. Avi Amar
lastPhoto.text:
Amidst the surrounding chaos, he kneels down, comforting a young soldier who struggled with the horrific sights on the ground with a fatherly embrace, creating a bubble of quiet for him.
Avi fell shortly after in the battle to regain control over Kibbutz Be'eri.
October 7, 2023. Aged 54 at his fall.

memorialSite.title: The Memorial Site - Riders' Post
memorialSite.text:
Near the Wadi in Moshav Otzem, his friends established a moving nature corner. On the sign, the words were dedicated: "The Riders' Post in memory of Chief Supt. Avi Amar, Hero of Israel: For man is like the tree of the field, like man the tree also grows, like the tree man is cut down".
The pastoral beauty of the place reflects his love for nature, horses, and life.

items.author: Pazit Amar
items.story: My beloved husband, it is still unbearable to think that you are no longer with us. I am sad and aching. I love you endlessly and miss you constantly. I know you will protect us forever.

items.author: Bat Sheva
items.story: My brother, the king of our family forever! It is inconceivable that you are no longer with us. You – the strongest, the hero, the protector and rescuer. You – the beautiful, the soft, and the noble. Not just your memory, but your reality and essence are with us at every moment. We speak of you and to you in the present, for we have no life without you. We all died on that cursed day you were taken from us, and we all live you in every remaining moment, until we meet again. Tearful, in pain, and refusing to believe.

items.author: Itay Amar
items.story: Zionism, the pursuit of excellence and the sanctity of life, these are the values by which Dad raised us. He took my sister and me to donate blood at Magen David Adom on our seventeenth birthday, and since then we've donated blood every three months because that's how he educated us. He was also the one who convinced me to enroll in the paramedics course.

candleWall.title: Virtual Candle Lighting
candleWall.subtitle: Here you can light a candle and leave a dedication in memory of Chief Supt. Avi Amar

form.messagePlaceholder: Share how Avi touched your life...

footer.inMemory: May his memory be a blessing - Dedicated with love and longing to the memory of {{name}}
